subject: [PATCH v7 3/3] mm: kick writeback flusher for IOCB_DONTCACHE with targeted dirty tracking

reviews.author: Christian Brauner
reviews.summary:
The reviewer pointed out a mistake in the patch, noting that test_and_set_bit() returns the old value and suggested removing the redundant test_bit() call.

Reviewer Christian Brauner noted that the filemap_dontcache_kick_writeback() function does not properly handle concurrent calls to wb_wakeup(), which can lead to a race condition between setting WB_start_dontcache and calling wb_wakeup().

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author agrees with the suggested improvement, asking if reviewer wants them to make the change or resubmit the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
That does look much cleaner. Do you want to just make that change or
would you rather I resend?

Thanks!

2026-05-11.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the writeback path in Linux by moving the submission of writeback work from the writer's context to a background flusher. This reduces lock contention and improves performance on systems using IOCB_DONTCACHE. The patch introduces a new WB_start_dontcache bit that allows for targeted dirty tracking, limiting the scope of expedited writeback while preserving I/O batching. The changes also include improvements to handling concurrent writes and cgroup writeback domain targeting.
